question: Return a table of order counts by payment_method and order_status, with columns payment_method, order_status, and order_count.
sql_query: SELECT p.payment_method, o.order_status, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count FROM core.payments p JOIN core.orders o ON p.order_id = o.order_id GROUP BY p.payment_method, o.order_status ORDER BY p.payment_method, o.order_status
pandas_sql_query: SELECT payment_method, order_status, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY payment_method, order_count DESC, order_status
diff_preview: [left]:  [delivered, cancelled, returned, shipped, paid, created, delivered, cancelled, returned, shipped, paid, created, delivered, cancelled, returned, paid, shipped, created, delivered, cancelled, returned, shipped, paid, created, delivered, cancelled, returned, shipped, paid, created]

[right]: [cancelled, created, delivered, paid, returned, shipped, cancelled, created, delivered, paid, returned, shipped, cancelled, created, delivered, paid, returned, shipped, cancelled, created, delivered, paid, returned, shipped, cancelled, created, delivered, paid, returned, shipped]
